posture: Inform — a clean scan: no fresh, dated NSW-REZ development cleared the relevance bar in the 24 hours to Saturday morning. A Friday-into-weekend window is a thin one, and the free source register produced no new agency release, market notice, planning step or developer disclosure with a REZ nexus: EnergyCo's latest-updates feed showed nothing published after the window opened, and the New England network-operator, corridor-refinement and Central-West Orana pages carried no new dated material. Every live thread the radar holds moved earlier and is deduplicated out as a prior-digest carry-forward: Squadron Energy's decision not to proceed with the 426MW Boorolong wind project inside the New England REZ (16 July, carried yesterday) and the political fight it fed, with the state government calling it the 'first investment victim' of the Coalition's late-June pledge to scrap the zone and its 500kV Bayswater-Walcha line and Squadron rejecting any policy motive; the New England Draft REZ Access Scheme Declaration still on public exhibition (feedback by 6 August, declaration targeted end-September) after EnergyCo's 14 July webinars, with the EIS expected to be exhibited in the second half of 2026; the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha corridor and its July-September field-investigation program across roughly 150 affected landholders, against live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition and the 22,000-signature petition debated in June; EnergyCo's 15 July South West REZ Project Development Deed with Transgrid (Jerilderie-Wagga Wagga, unlocking 3.56GW for the four access-rights projects) and Squadron's 14 July Banandra 330kV bypass; and on Central-West Orana the Port-to-REZ OSOM delivery campaign to Uungula, the Main Road No. 709 gazettal, and ACEREZ's staged access-track recommencement under stronger heritage controls with the DPHI rock-shelter assessment still open. Two source gaps are worth noting for today's run rather than treated as absence of news: ABC News and The Guardian Australia — the register's best free regional/community and political signals — were not retrievable by the automated fetcher in this run, and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, the ACM regional titles) were likewise unreadable even at headline level, so no paywalled items are carried today. Nothing in the scan changes the standing posture: the dominant exposures remain the New England political and social-licence contest ahead of the March 2027 state election, and the delivery and heritage risks on Central-West Orana.
summary: No fresh NSW-REZ-relevant item surfaced in the 24-hour window to Saturday 18 July morning — a clean scan across the free source register, with no new EnergyCo release, market notice, planning step or developer disclosure carrying a REZ nexus. All active threads are prior-digest carry-forwards with no movement in the window: the Squadron Boorolong exit and the New England political fight around the Coalition's plan to scrap the 500kV line, the New England Access Scheme exhibition, the July-September corridor field investigations and Walcha opposition, the 15 July South West REZ Transgrid export-upgrade deed, and the Central-West Orana OSOM campaign, Main Road No. 709 gazettal and ACEREZ heritage matter. ABC News, The Guardian Australia and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, ACM titles) could not be retrieved in this run and are logged as a source gap rather than a clear signal.
